title: Karaikal Ammayar Temple
tagline: Karaikal Ammayar Temple — spiritual town of Puducherry

overview.short: Karaikal Ammaiyar Temple (French : Pagode Somanadassamy or Pagode Karikal Amméar) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the Shaivite saint or Nayanmar Punithavathi, more famously known as
overview.description: Karaikal Ammaiyar Temple (French : Pagode Somanadassamy or Pagode Karikal Amméar) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the Shaivite saint or Nayanmar Punithavathi, more famously known as Karaikal Ammaiyar (transl. The revered mother of Karaikal). It is located on Bharathiar Street (Formerly the Rue de Poréar, or Poraiyar Street), in the center of Karaikal city, in India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kailasanathar Temple, Karaikal
topPlaces.description: Kailasanathar Temple (French : Pagode Caïlachanadar) is a Shaivite temple located in the downtown of Karaikal, India. It is among the prominent Hindu temples of the city, situated opposite to the famed Karaikal Ammaiyar Temple.

topPlaces.name: Nithyakalyana Perumal Temple, Karaikal
topPlaces.description: Nithyakalyana Perumal Temple is one of three famous temples in the port city of Karaikal, Puducherry, India. It is nearby the Karaikal Ammayar Temple.

topPlaces.name: Aayiram Kaliamman Temple
topPlaces.description: Aayiram Kaliamman Temple is an ancient temple in Tirumalairayanpattinam (T.R.Pattinam). The temple is much famous for the main deity, Goddess Kaliamman's statue is made up of wood.

topPlaces.name: Our Lady of Angels Church, Karaikal
topPlaces.description: Our Lady of Angels Church (also called Karaikal Church) is the oldest church in Karaikal in the Union territory of Pondicherry, in Southern India. The current structure was built in Neo-Gothic architecture by the French Colonial Empire in 1822.

image.alt: Our Lady of Angels Church, Karaikal

topPlaces.name: Karaikal
topPlaces.description: Karaikal (, Tamil: [kaːɾɐi̯kːaːl], French: Karikal [kaʁikal]) is a port city of the Indian Union Territory of Puducherry. It is the administrative headquarters of the Karaikal District and the second most populated town in the Union Territory after Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Karaikal taluk
topPlaces.description: Karaikal taluk is a taluk of Karaikal District of Puducherry, India. It consists of one municipality, Karaikal and the following revenue villages: Poovam Thiruvettakudy Varichikudy Kil Vanjiyur Mel Vanjiyur Keezhaiyur Polagam Apart from the above revenue villages, Karaikal taluk includes the following commune panchayats.

topPlaces.name: Karaikal Municipality
topPlaces.description: Karaikal Municipality is a municipality in the union territory Puducherry, India. It serves the area covered by the town of Karaikal, located in Karaikal taluk of Karaikal district.

topPlaces.name: Dano-Carical Conflict
topPlaces.description: The Dano-Carical Conflict (Danish: Konflikten mod Carical) was a small-scale conflict between the Danes at Tranquebar and the Portuguese at Carical (Karaikal). The conflict includes three smaller naval engagements, which eventually led to a four-hour-long imprisonment of Danish Captain Simon Jansen.

items.note: Kailasanathar Temple (French : Pagode Caïlachanadar) is a Shaivite temple located in the downtown of Karaikal, India. It is among the prominent Hindu temples of the city, situated opposite to the famed Karaikal Ammaiyar Temple.
Nithyakalyana Perumal Temple is one of three famous temples in the port city of Karaikal, Puducherry, India. It is nearby the Karaikal Ammayar Temple.
Aayiram Kaliamman Temple is an ancient temple in Tirumalairayanpattinam (T.R.Pattinam). The temple is much famous for the main deity, Goddess Kaliamman's statue is made up of wood.

items.activity: Our Lady of Angels Church, Karaikal
items.note: Our Lady of Angels Church (also called Karaikal Church) is the oldest church in Karaikal in the Union territory of Pondicherry, in Southern India. The current structure was built in Neo-Gothic architecture by the French Colonial Empire in 1822.

Karaikal (, Tamil: [kaːɾɐi̯kːaːl], French: Karikal [kaʁikal]) is a port city of the Indian Union Territory of Puducherry. It is the administrative headquarters of the Karaikal District and the second most populated town in the Union Territory after Pondicherry.

Karaikal taluk is a taluk of Karaikal District of Puducherry, India. It consists of one municipality, Karaikal and the following revenue villages: Poovam Thiruvettakudy Varichikudy Kil Vanjiyur Mel Vanjiyur Keezhaiyur Polagam Apart from the above revenue villages, Karaikal taluk includes the following commune panchayats.
Karaikal Municipality is a municipality in the union territory Puducherry, India. It serves the area covered by the town of Karaikal, located in Karaikal taluk of Karaikal district.

The Dano-Carical Conflict (Danish: Konflikten mod Carical) was a small-scale conflict between the Danes at Tranquebar and the Portuguese at Carical (Karaikal). The conflict includes three smaller naval engagements, which eventually led to a four-hour-long imprisonment of Danish Captain Simon Jansen.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Karaikal Ammayar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Karaikal Ammayar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Karaikal Ammayar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (126 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Karaikal Ammayar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Karaikal Ammayar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Karaikal Ammayar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Karaikal Ammayar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Karaikal Ammayar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kailasanathar Temple, Karaikal, Nithyakalyana Perumal Temple, Karaikal, Aayiram Kaliamman Temple, Our Lady of Angels Church, Karaikal, Karaikal.
